description: Extracts structured data from PDF, PNG, JPG, and JPEG documents using LLMs. Supports two modes: (1) dynamic mode, where the toolkit automatically identifies the correct parser template by analysing the document content against a vendor reference database — use this when you do not know which template to use; and (2) direct mode, where a specific known parser configuration is applied immediately — use this when you already know the exact template and their corresponding parser ID or config for the document.

capabilities.name: dynamic_template_llm_parser
capabilities.description: USE THIS when you do not know which parser template applies to the document. This capability first sends the document to an LLM to extract identifying information (e.g. company name, layout features), then queries the vendor reference database to find a matching parser template, and finally runs that matched parser to extract structured data. Requires a dynamic parser config ID (the orchestrator config, not a specific vendor parser). Fails with 'Unapproved' if no vendor match is found in the database.

config.description: The numeric ID or JSON file path of the parser config (e.g. '7841'). This is the top-level config that manages vendor matching, NOT a vendor-specific parser ID.

source.description: Absolute path to the source PDF, image file, or directory of files to parse.

env_file.description: Path to the .env file for this client

capabilities.name: llm_parser
capabilities.description: USE THIS when you already know the exact template and its exact parser ID or config for the document. This capability skips vendor identification and directly applies the specified LLM parser configuration to extract structured data from the document. Faster than dynamic_template_llm_parser because it makes one fewer LLM API call. Do NOT use this if the correct parser or template is unknown is unknown — use dynamic_template_llm_parser instead.

config.description: The numeric ID of config id or JSON file path of the specific parser config to apply directly (e.g. '7840'). Must match an active entry in the parser_config table.

source.description: Absolute path to the source PDF, image file, or directory of files to parse.

env_file.description: Path to the .env file for this client

capabilities.name: checking_timeout
capabilities.description: Use this for checking out the timeout. Loops and prints the current time at intervals. Runs for duration_minutes if provided, otherwise defaults to 2 minutes.

duration_minutes.description: How many minutes to run the loop. Leave empty or omit to default to 2 minutes.

capabilities.name: checking_env
capabilities.description: Use this for checking out environment variables present. It will list all the env variables that are accessable in the external toolkit.

author: HachiAI
